beats.speaker: ENV
beats.text: The ship is not beautiful. It was not built to be beautiful — it was built to move in deep water and survive what deep water does. The keel is Gnome-processed Hieronymus iron. It sits in the shallows at first light and it is the most real thing you have made with your hands.

beats.speaker: ENV
beats.text: A day out. The plateau has dropped below the horizon when the weather comes in fast from the west — the kind that has no warning and no interest in your plans. The ship holds for longer than it should. Then it does not hold.

beats.speaker: ENV
beats.text: The water is not cold the way cold usually is. It is absolute. You go under once. When you surface, the ship is already past you.

beats.speaker: ENV
beats.text: Something lifts you. Not a wave — something deliberate, underneath. You are deposited on a shelf of rock that was not there before. Beside you, a figure with gold and turquoise scales is doing the same for someone else — someone pulled from the wreckage who is breathing, barely.

beats.speaker: ENV
beats.text: The Undine watches you find your feet. There is something in her expression that is not urgency and not amusement. It is the face of someone who has seen this particular kind of arrival many times and finds it meaningful each time regardless.

opening_beats.speaker: 1009_UNDI
opening_beats.text: Gratitude is not necessary. I was here when you fell. Being here when things fall is part of what I do. My name is Faygoru.

opening_beats.speaker: 1009_UNDI
opening_beats.text: The other one is Chazak. He does not need your thanks either, but he will appreciate it if you offer it anyway. He was meditating when the storm came and he considers interruption a form of information.

beats.speaker: 1009_UNDI
beats.text: I knew the storm. I knew what it would do to a ship with that particular keel design in water of this depth. The rest was arithmetic.

beats.speaker: 1009_UNDI
beats.text: Between. The plateau does not reach here and the depths do not start here. It is a useful kind of place. Things arrive here that are not yet what they are going to be.

topics.prompt: Ask about what he was meditating on

opening_beats.speaker: 1010_SALA
opening_beats.text: Nothing. That is what Transcendental Meditation is — the practice of meditating on nothing and finding that nothing is not empty. It is the practice of sitting in the space before a thought arrives and discovering that space has texture.

opening_beats.speaker: 1010_SALA
opening_beats.text: You will need this. Not because the path ahead is difficult — it is — but because difficulty that you meet with a full mind breaks the mind. Difficulty met with empty space passes through.

beats.speaker: 1010_SALA
beats.text: Sit. Not because sitting is the meditation — it is not. Sit because the posture signals to everything else that you are choosing this, not falling into it.

beats.speaker: ENV
beats.text: The lesson takes the remainder of the day. By evening you can feel the difference — not in your thoughts, but in the space between them.

responses.text: What does Moshize have to do with this?

beats.speaker: 1010_SALA
beats.text: Moshize is what this practice is in relationship to the divine. The practice exists whether or not you know Moshize exists. But knowing Moshize makes the practice larger.
